title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot
summary: Headline PPI near-flat after May's energy shock. Hormuz deal opened the strait; WTI collapsed to $74, gasoline retail fell to $4.05. Energy PPI mean-reverts to -2.0%. Core PPI stays elevated at +0.5% m/m due to lagged petrochemical passthrough from May's +10.7% energy spike and sticky services. Stage 1 intermediate demand +3.2% from May still feeds into final demand goods. The headline is almost entirely an energy story; core is the real signal.
baseCase: Flat headline, sticky core; energy mean-reversion masks upstream persistence

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: Energy PPI mean-reverts after Hormuz reopening, but core goods lagged passthrough and sticky services keep core elevated. The headline is an energy mirage; the Fed cares about core.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1%, core +0.5%. Energy mean-reverts, core stays sticky.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates digest the headline but focus on core. No major Fed repricing. Cyclicals stabilize if margin narrative holds.

scenarios.label: Hotter pipeline pressure
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.4%, core +0.7%. Lagged passthrough hits harder than expected; services stay hot.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Rates higher, USD firmer, margin-sensitive equities under pressure. Fed hawkishness extends.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print is driven by narrow commodity volatility with weak pass-through into CPI.

scenarios.label: Cleaner disinflation pulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.2%, core +0.3%. Energy collapses further; core passthrough fades faster than expected.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Fed-cut odds rise, rates soften, broader equity appetite improves. Margin relief narrative strengthens.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome before release.

setups.bias: Long duration if core PPI cools without hawkish spillover
setups.trigger: Core PPI <= +0.3% m/m and revisions are benign
setups.invalidation: Broad upstream re-acceleration with hawkish rates repricing

setups.bias: Constructive only if margin pressure eases
setups.trigger: Core goods and services pipeline both avoid upside surprise
setups.invalidation: Input cost shock restarts (Hormuz re-closes, energy spikes)

setups.bias: Long on hotter PPI surprise with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline and core both beat with higher terminal-rate pricing

watchItems.label: Final demand services
watchItems.note: A hot services print can overwhelm softer goods. Portfolio management and healthcare are slow-moving but sticky.

watchItems.label: Commodity complex
watchItems.note: Important for headline direction but not enough alone. WTI $74 is a floor until Hormuz stability is confirmed.

watchItems.label: Pass-through
watchItems.note: Focus on whether PPI changes the CPI path or earnings narrative. Stage 1-4 intermediate demand is the leading indicator.

keyDrivers: Goods deflation helps, but services pipeline matters more for readthrough.
Commodity moves can distort the headline without changing core pass-through.
PPI matters most when it shifts margin expectations or the CPI narrative.
Hormuz reopening is a temporary supply shock reversal; geopolitical risk premium can return quickly.
risks: Consensus may already expect a benign upstream print after Hormuz deal.
Revisions can dominate the first market read. May 2026 was heavily revised.
Macro tape can be driven by yields or oil even if PPI itself is tame.
Iran deal is 60-day; collapse risk re-energizes energy PPI.
